donorReferences.adapted: packed RGBA traversal assumptions and bounded deterministic processing style
legacy alpha > threshold, 4-way flood-fill, stable scan order and 3x3/5-pixel minimum policy

contract.alphaMembership: alpha > alphaThreshold, with an integer threshold in [0,255]
contract.ordering: accepted components retain deterministic source row-major discovery order
contract.cancellation: optional synchronous predicate checked before work, per scan row and at flood head=0, 4096, 8192, ...

goldenCases: irregular L-shape and rectangle at source edge
fully transparent and zero-dimension empty source
single pixel retained by explicit policy and rejected by donor defaults
output ceiling failure without partial return
hostileCases: typed-array length accessor rejected without invocation
invalid and excessive dimensions rejected before traversal
cancellationCases: 1x2 transparent source cancels on the second row boundary after exactly 3 predicate calls
8193x1 opaque component cancels at flood head=4096 after exactly 4 predicate calls: pre-work, row, head=0, head=4096
the 8193x1 orientation stays within the 16384 dimension limit and avoids extra row-boundary predicate calls
the large-component case would return normally with only pre-work and row polling, so it specifically proves inner flood cadence
propertyInvariants: component pixel counts exactly partition alpha-eligible pixels
same input and policy returns identical frozen output
every returned bound fits inside the source

performance.result: passed in the focal Vitest run

independentReview.notes: The repaired evidence isolates row-boundary cancellation and proves the inner flood poll at head=4096 with exact predicate-call counts.

verification.details: 1 file, 10 tests; 432 ms test body; 4.80 s total duration

verification.command: $ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'; Get-Content artifacts/quality/GRID/2026-07-16/s1-01-connected-components.json -Raw | ConvertFrom-Json | Out-Null; git diff --no-index --check -- NUL tests/contract/irregularRegionDetection.test.ts; if ($LASTEXITCODE -gt 1) { exit $LASTEXITCODE }; git diff --no-index --check -- NUL artifacts/quality/GRID/2026-07-16/s1-01-connected-components.json; if ($LASTEXITCODE -gt 1) { exit $LASTEXITCODE }; exit 0
verification.details: JSON parsed; wrapper exit 0; no whitespace diagnostics (only Git LF-to-CRLF normalization warnings)

knownRisks: The adapter is intentionally synchronous; live cancellation during a CPU-bound call requires the caller to provide a predicate backed by worker-visible state.
The 16,777,216-pixel flood-fill cap is stricter than the general grid protocol to bound visited/frontier allocation.
